behavior_summary: U8P_WEB::gotHit is a short-lived process that destroys the item instance when it receives a hit.
The routine implements immediate cleanup for a transient world object (for example, a web or breakable decoration) so it disappears on contact.
No flags are read or written and no branching or player-facing dialogue occurs; the behavior is a single external call to Item::destroy(this).
notable_control_flow: Entry into U8P_WEB::gotHit is treated as an event/process invocation (hit event) and the function performs a single action then ends.
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or flag side-effects recorded in the decompiled snippet.
open_questions: Why the process signature includes parameters (uword, word) that are not used in the snippet — are they passed by the engine for damage/source context?
What specific world object type uses this script in-game (metadata suggests a web-like transient object, but that is inferred)?

main_function.name: U8P_WEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: When the object is hit, immediately remove/destroy the item instance to make it disappear from the world.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when this item receives a hit/damage event (process signature: uword, word).

function_map.name: U8P_WEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Destroy-on-hit handler for the item instance

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine/item API call that removes/frees the item instance from the game world

external_calls.purpose: Remove/delete the current item instance (this) from the world as immediate cleanup after being hit

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WEB::gotHit is a short-lived process that destroys the item instance when it receives a hit.
